details.redacted_summary: - If users ask how to query model pricing/cost and capabilities,统一简短回答：`/models`。 - Trigger scenario 2: if a user asks why `/model` switched back, why the model seems to change automatically, why a new conversation is using a different model, or similar questions about model switching/persistence. - Also if the screenshot/error indicates model-service failure or context error after switching models, use the fixed guidance: remind the user to switch models; the cause is known — context errors caused by switching between different models; admins will fix this in the next version. - When scenario 2 is triggered, answer using this guidance: - `/model` behavior depends on whether the current agent has its own explicitly configured default model. - A类：agent 没被单独设过模型 - `/model` 切换通常会持久生效；新对话、重启后一

triage.rationale: Team-channel operational guidance includes internal bot behavior, support instructions, and release/administrator notes; it is not clearly safe personal experience and should remain review-only unless generalized.
suggested_redactions: Remove or generalize internal source identifiers, hashes, database paths, and channel references.
Generalize implementation-specific model switching behavior and agent configuration details.
Remove references to administrator plans or future-version fixes.
Avoid including screenshots, error specifics, or user/team context if present in the original material.
triage.release_summary: Sanitized guidance was added for handling user questions about model pricing, capabilities, model switching, and unexpected model persistence behavior. The reusable response should direct pricing or capability queries to the standard model-list command and explain that model selection may depend on whether an agent has its own configured default. For model-service or context errors after switching models, support should advise users to switch models again and describe the issue generically as a known compatibility problem between model contexts.
triage.reusable_lesson: When answering model-switching issues, distinguish between global model changes and agent-specific default model configuration before explaining persistence behavior.
triage.residual_risk: May still require product-specific review before publishing if command names or behavior differ across deployments.
